utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , anna , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing okay . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm doing well . uh , so i know the nurse told you a little bit about dax . i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so , uh , anna is a 26-year-old female with a past medical history , significant for epilepsy , depression , and , uh , peptic ulcer disease , who presents with , uh , some joint pain . so , anna , what's going on with your , uh , what's going on with your joints ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so , um , it's my left elbow that's really been bothering me . uh , i sit at my desk a lot for work , and type , and i stay in this motion specifically all day . and then after , um , work , it's really hard for me to , uh , open and , and close my , my elbow here , my left elbow , and it's been just really stiff and , and hurts .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . have you noticed any swelling there on the-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i have .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and is it right here on the , on the tip ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep , it is .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and are you right or left-handed ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i am right-handed .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so it's your non-dominant hand ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , which is good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so , i can still write , which is great , but typing is , is difficult .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and any numbing or tingling in your hands at all ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i have n't felt numbing or tingling .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and any weakness in your arm ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a little bit of weakness , uh , but nothing too significant .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and ... and how about ..

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how about , um , any fever or chills ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no fever or chills .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , well , let's talk a little bit about your epilepsy , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , have you had any recent seizures ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , no , i have n't . i think my last seizure was a year ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and you're still taking the keppra ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . and then , tell me a little about your depression . how're you doing with that ? i know that you went into therapy last year , and you tried to avoid medication since you're already on the keppra . how's that doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , therapy , therapy's been good . it , it has definitely helped . uh , i still feel a little down , uh , and , and stressed .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , but no , no feelings of wanting to hurt yourself or somebody else ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and then , how are you doing with your , with your ulcer ? i know that you , you know , you had so much stress a year ago and , you know , you were having some issues there . we did the endoscopy-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . and they showed that . so , how are you doing with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing , i'm doing better . i have n't had any , um , issues with that , um , since we did the procedure , and everything's been good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , well great . let's go ahead and ... i'm gon na go ahead with , um ... and , you know , i know that the nurse did a review of systems sheet on you when you came in , and i know that you were endorsing that left elbow pain .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any other symptoms , you know , chest pain , shortness of breath , abdominal pain , nausea or vomiting ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that . i have a little bit of nasal , nasal congestion from allergies , uh , but that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so you're endorsing some nasal congestion ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , well , let's go ahead to a physical exam , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . good , here in the office , everything looks great with your vital signs . i think that's great , so i'm just gon na take a listen to your heart and your lungs , and take a look at your elbow and , and we'll go from there , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , so , so on physical examination of your left elbow , you do have some erythema and edema of your left elbow . does it hurt when i touch ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it does .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , he ... she has some pain to palpation of the olecranon . of the left olecra- olecranon , and it is warm to palpation . um , can you straighten your elbow for me ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it , it hurts , and it's , uh , it's a bit stiff .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so she has pain with flexion and extension of the left elbow . there's a palpable right ... or s- ... palpable left radial pulse . okay , um , so let's go over ... i just wan na take a look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the left elbow x-ray . hey , dragon , show me the elbow x-ray . hey , dragon , show me the left arm x-ray . okay , so , in reviewing the results of your left arm x-ray , there's no evidence of any elbow fracture , which is not surprising , okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the endoscopy results . hey , dragon , show me the endoscopy . okay , well , your recent endoscopy showed that you ... hey , dragon , show me the endoscope . all right , so here it looks like , you know ... your endoscopy results look like you had an episode of gastritis . that's for your , your peptic ulcer disease , and so everything , you know ... you took your , your protonix , and i think everything resolved at that point , okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , let's talk a little bit about my assessment and my plan for you . so for your first problem , this left elbow pain , you know , i think you have olecranon bursitis , and we're just gon na , you know , have you , you know , get a little pillow for your elbow there . it's just caused by repetitive trauma .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , maybe you're slamming your elbow down a little bit on the desk , so just get a little rest there-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . and we'll give you some melo- meloxicam , 15 mg , once a day . you can ice the area , okay ? um , for your next problem , the epilepsy , sounds like you're doing really well . i would go ahead and continue with the keppra , 500 mg , twice a day . do you need a refill of that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , order a refill of keppra , 500 mg , twice daily . for your third problem , the depression , it sounds like you're doing well with the therapy and , if you want , we can talk about medical treatment options .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i think that would be great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , well , let's go ahead and we'll start you on some lexapro , 10 mg , once a day , and we'll see how you do on that , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , and then , for your last problem , the peptic ulcer disease , um , i want you to continue on the protonix , 40 mg , once a day before meals , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you have any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i do n't .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , the nurse will come in soon and see you , okay , and i'll , i'll see you in a couple of weeks .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , take care . bye .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: bye .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 26-year-old female with a history of epilepsy, depression, and peptic ulcer disease.
Joint pain
The doctor asks the patient to describe her joint pain.
The patient reports left elbow pain, stiffness, and difficulty flexing or extending the joint after working at a desk.
The doctor asks if the patient has noticed any swelling in the elbow.
The patient confirms swelling in the elbow.
The doctor asks if the swelling is located on the tip of the elbow.
The patient confirms the swelling is on the tip of the elbow.
The doctor asks if the patient is right or left-handed.
The patient is right-handed.
The patient can write but finds typing difficult due to elbow pain.
The doctor asks if the patient has any numbness or tingling in her hands.
The patient denies numbness or tingling in her hands.
The doctor asks if the patient has any arm weakness.
The patient reports minor arm weakness.
The doctor asks if the patient has any fever or chills.
The patient denies fever or chills.
Epilepsy
The doctor asks if the patient has had any recent seizures.
The patient reports no recent seizures, with the last one occurring a year ago.
The doctor asks if the patient is still taking Keppra.
The patient confirms she is still taking Keppra.
Depression
The doctor asks how the patient is doing with her depression and therapy.
The patient reports therapy has been helpful, but she still feels down and stressed.
The doctor asks if the patient has any suicidal or homicidal ideations.
The patient denies suicidal or homicidal ideations.
Peptic ulcer disease
The doctor asks how the patient is doing with her peptic ulcer disease.
The patient reports no issues with her ulcer since the endoscopy procedure.
The doctor asks about other symptoms including chest pain, shortness of breath, abdominal pain, nausea, or vomiting.
The patient denies chest pain, shortness of breath, abdominal pain, nausea, or vomiting, but reports nasal congestion from allergies.
Physical exam
Physical examination of the left elbow reveals erythema and edema.
The patient has pain to palpation of the left olecranon and it is warm to palpation.
The doctor asks the patient to straighten her elbow.
The patient reports pain and stiffness with elbow movement.
The patient has pain with flexion and extension of the left elbow and a palpable left radial pulse.
The left arm x-ray shows no evidence of an elbow fracture.
The endoscopy results showed an episode of gastritis.
The doctor assesses the patient with left olecranon bursitis caused by repetitive trauma and recommends using a pillow to rest the arm.
The doctor prescribes meloxicam 15 mg once daily and recommends icing the area.
The doctor advises continuing Keppra 500 mg twice a day.
The doctor asks if the patient needs a refill of Keppra.
The patient confirms she needs a refill of Keppra.
The doctor asks if the patient wants to discuss medical treatment options for depression.
The doctor initiates Lexapro 10 mg once daily for depression.
The doctor advises continuing Protonix 40 mg once daily before meals for peptic ulcer disease.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.
The patient has no further questions.
